background: Exercise 13.1 presented data on birth weights of infants based on the smoking status of mothers. Table 13.3 lists the data. Descriptive statistics are shown in Table 13.10.

task.query: Conduct an ANOVA on these data. Show all hypothesis-testing steps. Step 1: State the hypotheses.
task.answer: Hypotheses. $H_0: \mu_1=\mu_2=\mu_3=\mu_4$ versus $H_3$: at least two of the $\mu_i$ s differ.

task.query: Conduct an ANOVA on these data. Show all hypothesis-testing steps. Step 2: Compute the test statistic.
task.answer:
Test statistic. ANOVA table (below), $F_{\text {stat }}=4.096 / 1.255=3.26$ with 3 and 21 df.

\begin{tabular}{lcrc}
\hline & Sum of Squares & $d f$ & Mean Square \
Between Groups & 12.289 & 3 & 4.096 \
Within Groups & 26.364 & 21 & 1.255 \
Total & 38.653 & 24 & \\ \hline
\end{tabular}

task.query: Conduct an ANOVA on these data. Show all hypothesis-testing steps. Step 3: Find the p-value.
task.answer: $P$-value. $P<0.05$ by Table D (use the 3 and $20 d f$ column). $P=0.042$ by a software utility. Either way, this provides good evidence against the null hypothesis.

task.query: Conduct an ANOVA on these data. Show all hypothesis-testing steps. Step 4: State the significance level and conclusion.
task.answer: Significance level (optional). The results are significant at $\alpha=0.05$ (reject $H_0$) but are not significant at $\alpha=0.01$ (retain $H_0$).
